name: 1Password Business
description: Enterprise password manager providing shared vaults, security policies and Watchtower credential-hygiene monitoring. Evidence covers access policies, security reports and audit logs — never the contents of vaults.

steps.action: Sign in to your account on 1Password.com, select Policies in the sidebar, and capture (screenshot or PDF) the account password requirements and sign-in policy settings
steps.security_note: Collect evidence of policy configuration and audit data only — never export or screenshot vault item contents
steps.audit_note: Policy screenshots evidence enforced authentication standards for the whole organisation

steps.action: Under Policies, capture the Two-Factor Authentication setting showing whether 2FA is allowed or enforced for the whole team (enforcement requires the Strict account password policy)
steps.audit_note: Evidence of enforced MFA supports access control requirements; note the enforcement date

steps.action: Select Reports in the sidebar and create a Business Watchtower report to capture weak, reused and compromised passwords and items missing two-factor authentication across shared vaults
steps.permissions_required: Owner, Administrator, or member of the Security team group
steps.audit_note: Watchtower reports aggregate credential-hygiene findings without exposing any credential values

steps.action: Create a usage report for key shared vaults (or specific team members) from the Reports area to show who has accessed or modified items during the collection period
steps.security_note: Usage reports show item names and access metadata only, not item contents — treat exports as confidential
steps.audit_note: Usage reports cover the last 12 months of activity

steps.action: Select Activity Log in the sidebar, filter to the collection period, and capture administrative events (policy changes, group membership changes, new device enrolments)
steps.audit_note: The audit log retains 365 days of events and entries are timestamped by 1Password — collect before events age out and ensure no gaps between collection periods

steps.action: Upload the exported reports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and evidence ID references
steps.security_note: Verify exports contain no credential values or bearer tokens before uploading

L2.title: 1Password Recurring Reports and Insights Review
L2.estimated_time: 30 minutes initial setup, then 10 minutes per cycle

steps.action: 1Password Business has no built-in scheduled report export or emailed report delivery — establish a recurring calendar task with a documented checklist as the closest reliable alternative
steps.audit_note: A documented, recurring checklist with completion records demonstrates a repeatable collection process

steps.action: On each cycle, review the Insights dashboard (Insights in the sidebar) covering password health, data breaches and team usage, and export or capture the summary
steps.permissions_required: Owner, Administrator, or member of the Security team group
steps.audit_note: Insights consolidates Watchtower findings account-wide, giving consistent period-on-period comparisons

steps.action: Create a domain breach report to identify company email addresses that appear in public data breaches, and use Notify Your Team for any affected members
steps.audit_note: Breach reports and notification records evidence proactive credential-exposure management

steps.action: Re-run the Business Watchtower report and vault usage reports on the same cadence, using standardised date-stamped filenames and a fixed evidence storage location
steps.security_note: Restrict the evidence storage location to authorised compliance personnel — reports contain access metadata about your team

steps.action: Record completion of each cycle in the evidence tracker, noting the period covered so consecutive collections leave no gaps
steps.audit_note: Consistent period coverage is key to the evidentiary value of recurring reports

steps.action: Sign in to your account on 1Password.com, select Integrations in the sidebar, and add an Events Reporting integration (choose Other to build your own Events API client)

Issue a bearer token for the integration: set a descriptive name, a 90-day expiry, and scope it only to the event types required (auditevents, signinattempts, itemusages), then use Save in 1Password to store it in a restricted vault
steps.security_note: Scope the token to the minimum event types needed and set an expiry rather than 'Never' — rotate before the expiry date
steps.audit_note: Token issuance is recorded in the account audit log

steps.action: Configure a scheduled script to call POST https://events.1password.com/api/v2/auditevents (and /api/v2/signinattempts, /api/v2/itemusages) with the Authorization: Bearer header, starting from a ResetCursor with a start_time, then persisting the returned cursor between runs (use events.1password.ca / events.1password.eu / events.ent.1password.com for accounts hosted in those regions)
steps.permissions_required: Bearer token scoped to auditevents, signinattempts and itemusages
steps.audit_note: Cursor-based pagination guarantees you resume exactly where the last run finished — no gaps and no duplicates

steps.action: Store the bearer token in a secrets manager and inject it as an environment variable at runtime — never hardcode it in scripts or config files
steps.security_note: The Events API returns access metadata only, never vault item contents, but the token still grants account-wide audit visibility and must be protected accordingly

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed collection runs, including alerts for HTTP 401 responses that indicate the token has expired or been revoked
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should be logged and trigger re-collection within SLA; the persisted cursor means missed periods are recoverable

steps.action: Validate collected data against expected volume — flag anomalies if daily sign-in attempt or audit event counts drop below baseline
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

steps.action: Select Integrations in the sidebar and add an Events Reporting integration for your SIEM (Splunk, Elastic, Sumo Logic, Datadog, Microsoft Sentinel and others are supported), issuing a scoped bearer token for the connector
steps.security_note: Save the bearer token in a restricted 1Password vault and reference it from the SIEM connector's credential store — never in pipeline config files

steps.action: Configure the SIEM-side collector to poll all three event streams (audit events, sign-in attempts, item usages) so the SIEM continuously ingests 1Password activity alongside your other audit sources
steps.permissions_required: SIEM administrator plus the scoped 1Password bearer token
steps.audit_note: Centralised ingestion puts 1Password access evidence under the SIEM's retention and immutability controls

steps.action: Configure collection completeness metrics in the SIEM: events per day by type, ingestion latency, and cursor lag between 1Password event time and SIEM index time
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Set up automated quality checks and alerting — flag gaps in the event time series, duplicate events, and approaching bearer token expiry so collection never silently stops
steps.security_note: Token expiry is the most common cause of silent collection failure — alert at least 14 days before expiry and rotate via the Integrations page

steps.action: Build a dashboard showing collection health (events/day per stream, latency, days since last event) alongside security posture indicators such as failed sign-in attempts and policy changes
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of both collection health and access monitoring

steps.action: Implement an automated quarterly review that generates a collection health report, confirms event stream coverage against the account audit log, and flags any SLA breaches
steps.audit_note: Reconciling SIEM data against the in-product audit log (365-day retention) validates end-to-end completeness
